tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128"

define i64 @multi_index_gep_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %i, i64 %j) {
  %gep = getelementptr nuw [0 x [0 x i32]], ptr %base, i64 %i, i64 %j
  %p2int_gep = ptrtoint ptr %gep to i32
  %zext_gep = zext i32 %p2int_gep to i64
  %p2int_base = ptrtoint ptr %base to i64
  %sub = sub i64 %zext_gep, %p2int_base
  ret i64 %sub
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128"

define i64 @multi_index_gep_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %i, i64 %j) {
  %offset = mul i64 %i, 0
  %offset2 = add i64 %offset, %j
  %offset3 = shl i64 %offset2, 2
  %ext = zext i64 %offset3 to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions GEP offset extraction but doesn't explicitly cover multi-index GEPs (2+ indices beyond the base). The patch handles any GEP operator regardless of index count, but summary focuses on simple single-offset patterns.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128"

define i64 @multi_index_gep_nuw(ptr %base, i64 %i, i64 %j) {
  %1 = ptrtoint ptr %base to i64
  %zext_gep = and i64 %1, 4294967295
  %p2int_base = ptrtoint ptr %base to i64
  %sub = sub i64 %zext_gep, %p2int_base
  ret i64 %sub
}
